ail: verb. To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)
all: determiner. Every individual or anything of the given class, with no exceptions (the noun or noun phrase denoting the class must be plural
ally: verb. To unite or form a connection between (people or things), as between families by marriage, or between states by confederacy, league,
ivy: noun. Any of several woody, climbing, or trailing evergreen plants of the genus Hedera.
lay: verb. To place down in a position of rest, or in a horizontal position.
lily: noun. Any of several flowers in the genus Lilium of the family Liliaceae, which includes a great many ornamental species.
sail: noun. A piece of fabric attached to a boat and arranged such that it causes the wind to drive the boat along.
sau: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Saleman.
saul: noun. Archaic form of sal (“the tree, Shorea Robusta”).
sial: noun. The rocks rich in silicon and aluminum that form the upper layer of the earth's crust, which lies beneath all continental landmasses.
sill: noun. A breast wall; window breast; horizontal brink which forms the base of a window.
silly: adjective. Laughable or amusing through foolishness or a foolish appearance.
silva: noun. The forest trees of a particular area
sly: adjective. Artfully cunning; secretly mischievous; wily.
sully: verb. To soil or stain; to dirty.
syli: noun. The currency of Guinea between 1971 and 1985, subdivided into 100 cauris.
ulva: noun. Any organism of the genus Ulva.
vas: noun. A vessel or duct transporting any bodily fluid, such as blood, lymph, chyle, or semen.
via: noun. A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
vial: noun. A glass or plastic vessel or bottle, especially a small tube-shaped bottle used to store medicine, perfume or other chemicals.
vill: noun. The smallest administrative unit of land in feudal England, corresponding to the Anglo-Saxon tithing and the modern parish.
villa: noun. A house, often larger and more expensive than average, in the countryside or on the coast, often used as a retreat.
villus: noun. A small projection from a membrane, particularly those found in the mucous membranes of the intestines.
visa: noun. A permit to enter and leave a country, normally issued by the authorities of the country to be visited.
visual: adjective. Related to or affecting the vision.
aul: noun. A village encampment in the Caucasus, Central Asia or the Southern Urals.
ayllu: noun. A traditional family-based community in the Andes, especially among Quechua and Aymara peoples.
ayu: noun. A sweetfish, an amphidromous game fish of East Asia of species Plecoglossus altivelis, prized for its sweet-tasting flesh and subject
islay: noun. Prunus ilicifolia, a traditional Native American food plant in southern California.
lai: noun. A mostly North European medieval form of lyrical, narrative poem written in octosyllabic couplets that often deals with tales of
lias: noun. A type of blue limestone present in parts of southern England and Wales.
lila: noun. The play of god’s spirit manifested in the universe; divine play, creative spiritual energy.
lulav: noun. A festive bouquet of willow, myrtle and a closed date palm frond, used in Jewish ritual on Sukkot.
sai: noun. A handheld weapon with three prongs, used in some Oriental martial arts.
saily: adjective. Resembling a sail, or sailing.
sil: noun. A yellowish pigment used by painters in ancient times.
silyl: noun. The radical SiH 3 derived from silane
siva: noun. Any of various birds formerly of genus Siva, now part of genus Actinodura, of barwings.
sull: verb. Of an animal: to stop; to refuse to go on.
sulla: noun. A Muslim man; especially one from Pakistan.
ull: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ullatan.
valyl: noun. The univalent radical derived from valine.
vila: noun. A type of female nature spirit in Slavic mythology, similar in some ways to a fairy or nymph.
vly: noun. A small swampy or marshy area, especially near a stream.
yali: noun. A type of house, particularly found on the Bosporus.
